LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BETTY NGUYEN'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND CROSS COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THE PROPOSED AMENDMENTS DO NOT CHANGE THE TENOR OF THE CASE AND THERE IS NOT SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE THAT THE AMENDMENTS ARE BEING RAISED IN BAD FAITH. DEPARTMENT 206 WILL DETERMINE WHETHER A TRIAL CONTINUANCE IS APPROPRIATE. THE COURT DENIES PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR CONDITIONS. AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT TO BE FILED BY NOVEMBER 14, 2012. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF AMIR MORTAZAVI'S MOTION FOR IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS IS DENIED. THE MOTION FAILS FOR BOTH PROCEDURAL AND SUBSTANTIVE REASONS. PROCEDURALLY, THE MOTION APPEARS TO BE A REPRISE OF PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION MADE TO DEPARTMENT 610 AND APPARENTLY DENIED BY THAT DEPARTMENT. THUS THE MOTION IS AN IMPERMISSIBLE MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION. SUBSTANTIVELY, THE MOTION IS A WASTE OF SCARCE JUDICIAL RESOURCES. IF THE COURT HAD TO READ THIS MANY PAGES EVERY TIME THERE WAS A MINOR VIOLATION OF CASE MANAGEMENT RULES, THE COURT WOULD NEVER HAVE TIME TO ADDRESS THE MANY IMPORTANT MOTIONS MADE BY LITIGANTS EVERY DAY. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Nov-29-2012 Text Ruling

Ntc Of Motion For Leave To Amend Cross Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-13-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PAUL BREUCOP OF DIMITROU & ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 434-1194 appearing for the defendant;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BETTY NGUYEN'S Motion For Leave To Amend Cross Complaint - The motion to amend the cross-complaint is granted. The proposed amendments do not change the tenor of the case and there is not substantial evidence that the amendments are being raised in bad faith. Department 206 will determine whether a trial continuance is appropriate. The Court denies Plaintiff's request for conditions. Amended Cross-Complaint to be filed by November 14, 2012. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. = (302/MJM)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-04-2012 Set For Trial On Jan-07-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS
Off Calendar Jun-11-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF AMIR MORTAZAVI'S MOTION FOR IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS IS Denied. The motion fails for both procedural and substantive reasons. Procedurally, the motion appears to be a reprise of plaintiff's objection made to department 610 and apparently denied by that department. Thus the motion is an impermissible motion for reconsideration. Substantively, the motion is a waste of scarce judicial resources. If the court had to read this many pages every time there was a minor violation of case management rules, the court would never have time to address the many important motions made by litigants every day. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077
